supporting_works.title: The pharmacopoeia universalis or, complete encyclopedia of the materia medica; contained in the pharmacopoeias of London, Edinburgh, and Dublin as well as of all those of Europe and America ; and of the dispensatories, formularies and chemical works ... With an appendix ... / by A. J. L. Jourdan : Edited by J. Rennie.
A popular cyclopedia of modern domestic medicine : comprising every recent improvement in medical knowledge : with a plain account of the medicines in common use
supporting_works.creator: Imray, Keith.

supporting_works.title: Domestic medicine and surgery : with a glossary of the terms used therein / by J.H. Walsh.

Modern domestic medicine : a popular treatise, describing the symptoms, causes, distinction, and correct treatment of the diseases incident to the human frame, embracing the modern improvements in medicine, to which are added, a domestic materia medica, a copious collection of approved prescriptions, &c. &c,  The whole intended as a comprehensive medical guide for the use of clergymen, heads of families, emigrants etc. / by Thomas J. Graham.

Modern domestic medicine : a popular treatise, describing the symptoms, causes, distinction, and correct treatment of the diseases incident to the human frame embracing the modern improvements in medicine to which are added, a domestic materia medica a copious collection of approved prescriptions, &c. &c together with an appendix on the cold water system the whole intended as a comprehensive medical guide for the use of clergymen, heads of families, and emigrants, in the absence of their medical adviser / by Thomas J. Graham.

Cooley's cyclopaedia of practical receipts and collateral information in the arts, manufactures, professions, and trades, including medicine, pharmacy, hygiene, and domestic economy : designed as a comprehensive supplement to the pharmacopoeia and general book of reference for the manufacturer, tradesman, amateur, and heads of families.

Modern domestic medicine: : a popular treatise, illustrating the symptoms, causes, distinction, and correct treatment of the diseases incident to the human frame; embracing the modern improvements in medicine. To which are added, a domestic materia medica; a copious collection of approved prescriptions, &c. &c. The whole intended as a comprehensive medical guide for the use of clergymen heads, [sic] of families, and invalids. / By Thomas J. Graham, M.D. &c.

A cyclopedia of domestic medicine and surgery: being an alphabetical account of the various diseases incident to the human frame ... also, instructions for administering the various substances used in medicine; for the regulation of diet and regimen / by Thomas Andrew, with contributions by several members of the Royal College of Surgeons, Edinburgh.
A hand-book of domestic medicine : popularly arranged / by an eminent physician.

Dr. Mansperger's book of hygiene : containing instructions for the cure of diseases, together with many valuable and useful prescriptions : added to which is an accurate description of the properties and application of the curative powers of roots and herbs, with instructions for a diagnosis of diseases from the urine and pulse
supporting_works.creator: Mansperger, K.

overview.title: Herbal Simples Approved for Modern Uses of Cure
overview.section_heading: HERBAL SIMPLES APPROVED FOR MODERN USES OF CURE
overview.excerpt: "To wash the feet," said Gerard, "in a decoction of Henbane, as also the often smelling to the flowers, causeth sleep." Similarly famous anodyne necklaces were made from the root, and were hung about the necks of chil...

overview.title: The family and ship medicine chest companion : being a compendium of domestic medicine, surgery, and materia medica : with directions for the diet and management of the sick-room : particularly adapted for heads of families, captains of ships, travellers, and overseers of plantations : comprising plain rules for the administration of medicines, with their properties and doses : also general rules on bathing, a plain description of the treatment of fractures, and dislocations, etc., etc., and a concise account of the asiatic and spasmodic cholera, with the remedies to be used in the absence of professional assistance : to which is added receipts of general utility for family purposes : selected from standard works
overview.excerpt: When given in sufficient doses, henbane operates as a decided narcotic, and in its general effects, much resembles opium; but it does not constipate, or affect the head; on the contrary, its ten- dency is rather to re...

overview.title: Thomson's conspectus : adapted to the British pharmacopoeia / edited by Edmund Lloyd Birkett.
overview.excerpt: -Almost inodorous; taste sweet, austere; colour a deep reddish purple ; soon hardens and becomes brittle. Oper. —Astringent. Use. —In diarrhoeas ; the protracted stage of dysentery; and internal haemorrhages. It may b...

overview.title: A compendium of domestic medicine : and companion to the medicine chest comprising plain directions for the employment of medicines, with their properties and doses ... / by John Savory.
overview.excerpt: Cinnamon water, half an ounce. Laudanum, fifteen minims. Antimonial wine, twenty-five minims. Syrup of poppies, two drachms. Mix, and make a draught for rheumatism. Narcotic Pills. Extract of henbane, half a scruple....

overview.title: Cooley's cyclopaedia of practical receipts and collateral information in the arts, manufactures, professions, and trades, including medicine, pharmacy, hygiene, and domestic economy : designed as a comprehensive supplement to the pharmacopoeia and general book of reference for the manufacturer, tradesman, amateur, and heads of families.
overview.excerpt: The heat of the body gradually volatilises the acetic acid, and the escape of the vapour being prevented by the oil skin, a strongly counter-irritant action is set up. Derbyshire’s Embrocation. From opium and mottled...

retrieval_text: Henbane is a medicinal herb profile in the Herbalisti historical corpus. It is represented across 303 works and 1101 source-linked chunks. Recorded variants include Henbane. Common plant-part co-mentions include Leaf, Seed, Gum, Root, Flower, Bark. Common preparation co-mentions include Extract, Tincture, Pill, Powder, Oil, Juice. Common condition co-mentions include Cough, Cold or catarrh, Inflammation, Wounds or ulcers, Constipation, Rheumatism. Caution co-mentions include Dose caution, Narcotic or sedative caution, Allergy or sensitivity caution, Child-use caution, Bleeding caution, Toxicity watch. Representative source works include The pharmacopoeia universalis or, complete encyclopedia of the materia medica; contained in the pharmacopoeias of London, Edinburgh, and Dublin as well as of all those of Europe and America ; and of the dispensatories, formularies and chemical works ... With an appendix ... / by A. J. L. Jourdan : Edited by J. Rennie.; A popular cyclopedia of modern domestic medicine : comprising every recent improvement in medical knowledge : with a plain account of the medicines in common use; Domestic medicine and surgery : with a glossary of the terms used therein / by J.H. Walsh.; Modern domestic medicine : a popular treatise, describing the symptoms, causes, distinction, and correct treatment of the diseases incident to the human frame, embracing the modern improvements in medicine, to which are added, a domestic materia medica, a copious collection of approved prescriptions, &c. &c,  The whole intended as a comprehensive medical guide for the use of clergymen, heads of families, emigrants etc. / by Thomas J. Graham.. This is a retrieval summary of historical co-mentions only, not a medical claim.
